name: Gene Bertoncini
abstract: Gene J. Bertoncini (born 6 April 1937) is an American jazz guitarist.

about_quotes.speaker: Sheryl Bailey
about_quotes.evidence: I met him when I was a teenager... that was so influential in how I saw the guitar, which I credit to that moment of crossing paths with him... I hung out with him and took some lessons.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheryl Bailey interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 24, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Moore
about_quotes.evidence: my work with Gene Bertoncini... kept you two together for so many years... he gets jobs... featuring the bass in a very nice way
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Moore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Crow
about_quotes.evidence: Gene Bertoncini was chatting up a girl on the street while he was waiting for Bobby Shanken to come down, the drummer. And Gene is a very handsome guy and beautifully dressed usually, and very tidy. And when he told her that he was a guitar player, she didn't believe him. She said, “You don’t look l
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Crow interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: Gene Bertoncini quit the show and they asked me to do it with Skitch Henderson... Well Doc Severinsen was in the band... I’m sitting there with Al Klink...
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Clay Jenkins
about_quotes.evidence: Clay maintains a robust performing schedule and records with jazz artists such as Gene Bertoncini
